key_strengths: 75% recurring revenue from mission-critical VMS customers with near-zero historical churn
Switching costs embedded in 1,000+ niche software products — organizational lock-in, not contractual
Decentralized meta-moat: 100+ verticals, no single competitor can attack all simultaneously
Exceptional capital allocation discipline: 20–30% IRR hurdle rates held through multiple rate cycles
Asset-light model with capex under 0.2% of revenue; FCFA2S compounded at 16–18% CAGR for a decade
Mandatory insider share purchase program aligns executives with shareholders at every level
No meaningful customer concentration: 100,000+ end customers across six operating groups globally
key_risks: Mark Leonard's September 2025 health departure — first real succession test of capital allocation culture under Mark Miller
Q4 2025 FCFA2S declined 12% YoY — deceleration may extend into H1 2026, expanding the forward multiple
AI disruption of VMS end markets over a 5–10 year horizon, particularly Vela group (~30% of revenues)
Acquisition quality erosion as deal sizes increase to $100M+, reducing addressable universe and information advantage
Revolving credit facility ($1.085B) matures November 2026 — requires renewal under prevailing market conditions
PEMS minority investment strategy introduces new risk vector outside CSU's controlled full-acquisition model
red_flags: Q4 2025 FCFA2S declined 12% YoY — first actual quarterly FCF contraction on record
GAAP net income fell 30% in FY2025 ($512M vs $731M) — headline figure will continue to depress sentiment
Mark Miller unproven as independent capital allocator at scale — conviction relies on track record, not new data
PEMS strategy (minority stakes) departs from CSU's historically controlled full-acquisition model
buy_triggers: Q1 or Q2 2026 FCFA2S growth returns to 15%+ YoY, confirming Q4 2025 deceleration was transient
Stock falls to CAD 2,200–2,500 with no new fundamental deterioration, increasing price discount to 35–45%
Mark Miller's spring 2026 shareholder letter demonstrates capital allocation discipline consistent with Leonard's framework
sell_triggers: Organic maintenance and recurring revenue turns negative on a constant-currency basis for two consecutive quarters
FCFA2S declines for three consecutive quarters without a credible management explanation
Acquisition multiples paid rise materially to 3–4x revenue, suggesting hurdle-rate discipline has been quietly abandoned
valuation_summary: At CAD 2,939, CSU trades at ~37x trailing FCFA2S and ~26x forward FCFA2S (projected CAD 2.4B for 2026), representing a ~23% discount to Alpha Spread's base-case DCF of CAD 3,616 and ~30–35% below analyst consensus targets of CAD 4,200–4,365; the current price implies only 10–11% annual FCFA2S growth for 10 years — well below the 16–18% historical rate — suggesting the market is pricing in meaningful deceleration that may not materialize.
source_summary: Primary sources: GlobeNewswire Q4 2025 earnings release (March 9, 2026), Q4 2024 audited shareholder report (March 2025), Alpha Spread DCF, MarketBeat/TipRanks consensus, GuruFocus/StockAnalysis.com ratios, PortfoliosLab drawdown data; balance sheet figures are as of December 31, 2024 (latest audited), FY2025 income/FCF figures from unaudited press release.
